subject: Actor redesign.
commit_message:
Actor redesign.

Left original code intact so that it can continue to be used until
everything has been converted to use the new approach.  Simply added
new methods and classes.  (A few minor edits were required to the old
code, e.g., added constructors to the Actor implementations).  Code
to be removed is annotated with "TODO".

This only contains one revised actor, SDNC.  This actor combines code
from actor.sdnc, sdnc, and drools-applications.

Coverage tests are incomplete, but I anticipate some simplification
to this design in a couple of days; coverage will be added at that
time.

Issue-ID: POLICY-1625
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>
Change-Id: I4b75730e3621a9ee026ad10e557abe92df10dcf4

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2

Would like to have Bruno and Liam's comments for this if possible.

Patch Set 4:

This is a general comment and is something to consider for the future but ti would be nice to do it in Frankfurt.

Maybe at some point we should consider the module structure and package naming structure. I think models-interactions was a catch-all we brought in when we lifted this code from drools-applications. One problem at the moment is that it's very difficult to know which module code is in because the package names are not mapped to the module structure. I always struggle with this.

For example the package "org.onap.policy.controlloop.actor.appc" is in model-interactions/model-actors/actor-appc

A suggestion would be to have a new module called "model-actor" and move the actor code into it. with a new package name hierarchy "org.onap.policy.models.actor........".

Also are actors exclusively for control loops, could they not be used in open loop operations. If that is the case, then we would not need "controlloop" in the package hierarchy.

Maybe we can move the code into new modules as it's being restructured and leave the old code in the existing location, and when all the new code is ready and not being used anymore, we can cut over to it.

Patch Set 4:

(6 comments)

I'll review the tests in another comment.

Patch Set 4:

OK, I reviewed the tests, thanks for this great work Jim.

I'll see about removing control loop specifics from the code in a subsequent review.  If the actor operations are not control loop specific, then it would indeed make sense to move the packages around. Again, I'd do that in a subsequent review, but would like input from others that that is indeed appropriate.

Patch Set 4:

Open loops have no actors/operations - but I prefer to use the term controlloop. Of course these actors could be used in other subsequently policy types (eg NON controlloop situations). But agree to rename things in G-Release as we are terribly behind in this work and package renaming can get hairy.
Change has been successfully merged by Jim Hahn
